docs.text: ProjX User Guide GitHub Repository: https://github.com/pcr1304/sw_project ProjX is a domain-specific language for modeling projectile motion, running physics simulations, comparing trajectories, and generating interactive browser-based visualizations. With a compact .px syntax, it eliminates the need to stitch together separate calculation, plotting, and presentation tools. Target Audiences & Use Cases Students and instructors demonstrating kinematics and projectile motion. Developers prototyping physics experiments and bounce scenarios. Anyone looking to compare identical launches across different gravities visually. 1. Setup & Build Prerequisites OCaml 5.x opam (OCaml package manager) dune (build system) yojson (JSON generation) Installation Install OCaml and opam . Then install missing dependencies: bash opam install dune yojson Build the project: bash dune build Run the tests (testing lexer/parser integration): bash dune test 2. Syntax Basics A .px script consists of statements grouped via blocks { ... } . Semicolons are not used. Variables and projectiles must be declared before use. Comments Comments use the # character. Anything trailing # is ignored. # This is a comment let g = 9.8 Variables & Expressions Declare and update numeric variables. You can intermix expressions and parentheses. let v0 = 25 + 5 set v0 = v0 * 2 3. Projectile & Simulation Blocks Projectile Block Define a projectile setup. angle and speed are strictly required. projectile cannon { angle 45 # Required (degrees) angle_azimuth 30 # Optional horizontal aim (degrees) speed 70 # Required launch_from (20, 30, 40) # Optional start pos (x, y, z) mass 5.0 # Optional (for drag calculation) drag_coefficient 0.3 # Optional (for drag calculation) cross_section 0.05 # Optional (for drag calculation) } Simulate Block A simulate block executes a physics scenario and generates the plot. Rule: It must contain exactly one gravity and at least one plot statement. simulate { gravity 9.8 air_resistance true air_density 1.225 wind_y 4.0 # Optional X/Y/Z wind influence plot cannon # Draw the trajectory # Query Metrics range cannon max_height cannon max_range cannon max_rectangle cannon # Dynamic checks check range.cannon() > 200 collide cannon sweep } 4. Dot Queries Dot queries allow fetching derived values dynamically in conditional checks or variable assignments. range.ball() : Range of the projectile max_height.ball() : Max height achieved max_range.ball() : Theoretical max range max_rectangle.ball() : Maximum rectangle area beneath the arc min_vel.ball(x, h) : Minimum velocity needed to clear a target tower collide.(p1, p2) : Returns 1 if collision occurs, 0 otherwise min_dist.(p1, p2) : Reports the minimum distance between two arcs Example in context: let r = range.cannon() if range.cannon() > 100 and max_height.cannon() > 20 { let success = 1 } 5. Advanced Features Game Mode Swap a standard simulation for an interactive web game: game { planet earth # Valid: earth, moon, mars, jupiter, sun level 1 lives 3 } Comparing Scenarios (Forking) fork creates comparative simulation branches for an existing projectile. Each branch is effectively a simulation block requiring its own gravity and plot . fork cannon { branch "Earth" { gravity 9.8 plot cannon } branch "Moon" { gravity 1.6 plot cannon } } Control Flow Loops ( for , repeat , while ) and conditionals ( if/else ) are built-in. for a from 15 to 75 step 15 { projectile sweep { angle a speed 70 } simulate { gravity 9.8 plot sweep } } 6. Execution & the Compilation Pipeline When a .px program executes, it follows a systematic pipeline from raw input to generated graphical view: flowchart TD A([.px Source File]) --> B(Tokenizer: lib/tokenizer.ml) B --> C(Parser: lib/parser.ml) C --> D(Semantic Checker: lib/checker.ml) D --> E(Evaluator & Physics: lib/eval.ml & lib/physics.ml) E --> F(JSON Emitter: lib/json_emit.ml) F --> G(HTML Generator: bin/main.ml) G --> H([HTML Interactive UI]) Tokenizer ( lib/tokenizer.ml ): Converts raw text strings into distinct linguistic tokens. Parser ( lib/parser.ml ): Composes the sequences of tokens into an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST). Semantic Checker ( lib/checker.ml ): Validates variable scopes, projectile dependencies, and simulation rules. Evaluator & Physics ( lib/eval.ml , lib/physics.ml ): Evaluates queries, calculates kinematics, and processes interactions. JSON Emitter ( lib/json_emit.ml ): Bundles output metrics and positional data into a structured format for rendering. HTML Generator ( bin/main.ml ): Automates rendering all JSON properties neatly into a standalone interactive web application. Run your .px file: dune exec projx -- input/queries.px Outputs JSON to the console and generates an interactive web interface (e.g., input/queries.html ). Open the .html file inside any web browser for visualization.
docs.title: ProjX User Guide
